description: This conceptual illustration depicts a square area divided into three distinct land cover / land use (LULC) classes. The top-right portion, shaded dark green, is labelled "Woody". The central, light green band is labelled "Permanent herbaceous". The bottom-left area, coloured blue, is labelled "Water". This visual representation demonstrates the adjacency of these different land cover types within a single conceptual unit or pixel, aligning with class definitions for the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) CLC+ Backbone Raster Product. 'Woody' corresponds to CLMS class 5 (Low-growing woody plants), 'Permanent herbaceous' to class 6, and 'Water' to class 10.
